meta.name: SD3 Short Dark Triad
meta.description: The SD3 is a 27-item self-report scale that assesses the three dimensions of the Dark Triad: Machiavellianism, Narcissism, and Psychopathy. It is a concise and efficient measure widely used in personality psychology research.
meta.author: Jones & Paulhus (2014)
meta.reference: Jones DN, Paulhus DL. Introducing the Short Dark Triad (SD3): a brief measure of dark personality traits. Assessment. 2014;21(1):28-41.
meta.instruction: Please rate how much you agree with each statement.

questions.text: It’s wise to keep track of information that you can use against people later
Wait for the right time to get back at people rather than show anger immediately
Make sure your plans benefit yourself, not just others
Most people can be manipulated
You need to sweet-talk people sometimes to get what you want
It’s important to protect yourself when dealing with others
It’s better to have others do things for you than do them yourself
Most people are only out for themselves
Getting what you want is more important than being moral
People often say I’m a special person
I like to get acquainted with important people
I feel I’m more special than most people
I like to be the center of attention
I feel I am a born leader
I hate being just another face in the crowd
I have compared myself to famous people and felt just as talented
I like it when people pay special attention to me
I feel I deserve more respect than the average person
I like to get revenge on people
I tend to not think before I act in dangerous situations
Payback is a rule I follow
I rarely feel genuine remorse
If someone messes with me, I get back at them
Cutting corners may be necessary to succeed
People often say I’m too reckless
I seek out thrill-seeking experiences
People say I’m often insensitive

ranges.label: Low Dark Traits
ranges.description: Your overall dark personality traits are low, indicating that manipulation, self-aggrandizement, and callousness are not prominent features of your interpersonal style. You tend to approach others with sincerity and mutual respect.
suggestions: Continue nurturing your cooperative and empathic approach to relationships, as these qualities build lasting trust
Recognize that your low dark traits are a relational strength that supports healthy social bonds
Stay aware that being overly trusting can sometimes leave you vulnerable to exploitation by others

ranges.label: Moderate Dark Traits
ranges.description: Your dark personality traits fall within the average range. Most adults score in this band, reflecting a typical balance between self-interest and concern for others that is common in everyday social functioning.
suggestions: Reflect periodically on whether competitive or self-serving tendencies overshadow empathy in your interactions
Maintain clear ethical boundaries when pursuing personal goals, especially under pressure or high stakes
Strengthen relationships by actively practicing perspective-taking and genuine listening in conversations

ranges.label: Elevated Dark Traits
ranges.description: Your dark personality trait scores are elevated above average, suggesting a tendency toward strategic manipulation, self-centered thinking, or reduced empathy. These patterns may create friction in close relationships over time.
suggestions: Examine specific situations where you resort to manipulation and explore alternative approaches that respect others
Practice deliberate empathy exercises such as journaling from another person's perspective after conflicts
Seek feedback from trusted individuals about how your behavior affects them to gain honest external insight
Consider working with a therapist who specializes in interpersonal patterns to develop healthier strategies

ranges.label: High Dark Traits
ranges.description: Your dark personality traits are substantially elevated across one or more dimensions. This level of manipulativeness, grandiosity, or callousness can significantly impair relationship quality and long-term social adjustment.
suggestions: Seek professional psychological support to explore the roots and consequences of these personality patterns
Commit to honest self-examination about how your behavior impacts partners, friends, and colleagues
Learn and practice skills for building reciprocal trust, such as active listening and emotional validation
Set concrete behavioral goals for reducing manipulative or exploitative actions in daily interactions
Monitor impulsive or retaliatory urges and develop healthier coping strategies through structured practice

machiavellianism.label: Low
machiavellianism.description: You show low Machiavellianism, indicating you rarely use strategic manipulation or deception to advance your interests. You tend to be straightforward and value honesty in your dealings with others.

machiavellianism.label: Moderate
machiavellianism.description: Your Machiavellianism is at a moderate level, reflecting occasional pragmatic or strategic thinking in social situations. While you can be calculated when necessary, it does not dominate your relational approach.

machiavellianism.label: High
machiavellianism.description: You display high Machiavellianism, suggesting a strong inclination toward strategic manipulation and cynical views about human nature. This pattern may undermine trust and authenticity in your closer relationships over time.

machiavellianism.label: Very High
machiavellianism.description: Your Machiavellianism is very high, indicating pervasive use of manipulation, deception, and exploitation in interpersonal contexts. Consider reviewing your relational patterns with a professional to foster more genuine connections.

narcissism.label: Low
narcissism.description: You show low narcissism, suggesting you do not have an inflated sense of self-importance or a strong need for admiration. You are likely comfortable sharing the spotlight and valuing the contributions of others equally.

narcissism.label: Moderate
narcissism.description: Your narcissism is at a moderate level, reflecting a healthy degree of self-confidence and desire for recognition. While you enjoy acknowledgment, it does not excessively dominate your social behavior.

narcissism.label: High
narcissism.description: You display high narcissism, indicating a strong sense of entitlement, need for admiration, and tendency to view yourself as superior. This may lead to interpersonal conflicts when others do not meet your expectations for special treatment.

narcissism.label: Very High
narcissism.description: Your narcissism is very high, suggesting pervasive grandiosity, entitlement, and self-focus that likely strain your relationships. Consider reflecting honestly on how your need for admiration affects those around you.

psychopathy.label: Low
psychopathy.description: You show low psychopathy traits, indicating good impulse control, genuine remorse when you cause harm, and a general concern for the well-being of others. You are unlikely to engage in reckless or callous behavior.

psychopathy.label: Moderate
psychopathy.description: Your psychopathy traits are at a moderate level, reflecting some tolerance for risk and occasional emotional detachment. While you may sometimes act impulsively, you generally maintain social boundaries.

psychopathy.label: High
psychopathy.description: You display high psychopathy traits, suggesting reduced empathy, impulsive decision-making, and a retaliatory interpersonal style. These tendencies may lead to risky behavior and difficulty maintaining stable relationships.

psychopathy.label: Very High
psychopathy.description: Your psychopathy traits are very high, indicating pervasive callousness, impulsivity, and lack of remorse. Professional support is strongly recommended to address patterns that may harm both yourself and others.
